political.s: Stellantis reported $269,585 in PAC/candidate contributions and $2.44 million in lobbying spending in 2024. The company has engaged in bipartisan political activity typical of automakers, while also advocating to weaken emissions standards and supporting the Transportation Freedom Act, which would repeal existing GHG emissions standards.

charity.s: Stellantis launched a comprehensive Stellantis Philanthropy program focused on education and STEM initiatives. The company operates through multiple foundations including the FCA Foundation, providing educational grants and employee volunteerism programs with 18 hours of paid time off annually. Global initiatives partner with over 70 NGOs supporting educational projects.
charity.focus: Education, STEM, youth development, career guidance, community service

environment.s: Stellantis has faced significant emissions compliance violations, including a 2022 guilty plea to criminal conspiracy in diesel emissions fraud ($300 million penalty) and a $4.2 million 2024 settlement with California for using unapproved emissions control bypass devices. The company paid $190.6 million in CAFE standard penalties in 2025. Despite net-zero commitments by 2038, Stellantis has actively lobbied against emissions standards and endorsed the Transportation Freedom Act to repeal federal GHG standards.

labor.s: Stellantis has faced multiple NLRB cases involving unfair labor practices and union relations. Notably, General Motors filed fraud and bribery allegations against FCA US (Stellantis) and a former labor VP in May 2026 regarding a decade-long conspiracy to corrupt collective bargaining. CEO pay ratio reached 518X in 2023 (highest-paid employees earned far more than average workers), with CEO compensation growth averaging 32% since 2020 versus 5.5% for average employees.

dei.s: Stellantis has demonstrated strong pro-DEI commitment through its Dare Forward 2030 strategic plan targeting 30% female leadership by 2025 and 35% by 2030. The company earned top scores on the Disability Equality Index for three consecutive years and maintains 11 Business Resource Groups supporting LGBTQ+, disability, women, veteran, and minority communities across 130 markets and 170 nationalities.

guns.s: N/A - automotive manufacturer; manufactures vehicles including Ram trucks and Jeep vehicles which may be used for various purposes including those with firearms access

privacy.s: Limited public information available on Stellantis' comprehensive data privacy practices and policies. The company operates globally across 130 markets with significant data collection through connected vehicles and customer services, but detailed privacy policies and third-party assessments are not readily available.

execPay.s: Stellantis exhibits extreme executive pay disparity. CEO pay ratio was 518X in 2023 (primarily from transformation incentive), declining to approximately 365X in 2022 baseline. CEO compensation grew 32% average since 2020 while average employee compensation grew only 5.5%. Current CEO Antonio Filosa earned $6.37 million in 2024 while average worker earns significantly less.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
